channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: APEC 2026 Forum in Shanghai Focuses on Regional Food Safety and Innovation
item.description: The 12th APEC Food Safety Cooperation Forum (FSCF) opened in Shanghai on 12 May 2026, bringing together delegates from 15 APEC member economies, international organisations, and diplomatic missions to...

item.title: Interior Designer Urges King Charles to Reside at Buckingham Palace Following £369 Million Renovation
item.description: The refurbishment of Buckingham Palace, an ambitious £369 million project involving 775 rooms, remains on track, with floorboards replaced, the Grand Staircase under scaffolding, and cornicing cracks ...

item.title: UK’s fortnightly bin collections show benefits for recycling, waste reduction and cost savings
item.description: The UK has successfully implemented a shift from weekly to fortnightly collection of residual waste, while maintaining weekly food-waste collections. This approach, developed over two decades of pilot...

item.title: Full list of PlayStation Plus games for May 2026 revealed
item.description: Sony has unveiled the lineup of games coming to its PlayStation Plus service for May 2026, covering both the Premium and Extra subscription tiers. Subscribers will be able to access these titles from ...

item.title: Zanzibar Uses Drones and AI to Eliminate Malaria by Targeting Mosquito Breeding Sites
item.description: Zanzibar imeshaanzisha project mpya ya ku fight malaria kwa kutumia drones na AI. The initiative, inayoitwa 'Smart Drone Technology for a Malaria-Free Future in Zanzibar', ni collaboration kati ya Zan...

item.title: Union Officers’ Lighthearted “Brawl” Captured in 1863 Civil War Photograph
item.description: In April 1863, photographer James F. Gibson, often associated with the renowned Mathew Brady studio, captured a rare and humorous moment at the Union Army headquarters in Falmouth, Virginia. Titled 'A...

item.title: Toronto Tempo secure first franchise victory with 86-73 win over Seattle Storm
item.description: The Toronto Tempo, Canada’s new WNBA expansion team, achieved a milestone by recording the first win in franchise history, defeating the Seattle Storm 86-73 at Coca-Cola Coliseum in front of 8,142 fan...

item.title: Njugush Borrows Abel Mutua’s SUV, Turns Market Trip into Comedy Sketch
item.description: Kenyan comedian Njugush, whose real name is Timothy Kimani, has sparked reactions online after borrowing his best friend Abel Mutua’s Land Rover Discovery. Abel, who bought the luxury SUV in 2023 afte...

item.title: ICE deploys pepper spray on detainees amid severe overcrowding at Arizona facility
item.description: In late February 2026, a serious incident occurred at the Arizona Removal and Operations Coordination Center (AROCC) in Mesa, Arizona, when ICE agents used pepper spray on 47 detainees confined in a r...

item.title: NBI driver detained after gunshots fired inside Senate premises in Pasay City
item.description: A 44-year-old Filipino man, known by the alias 'Mel Oragon' and employed as a driver at the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI), was arrested following multiple gunshots fired inside the Philippine...

item.title: Rivers State University Opens ICT Centre to Boost Digital Skills and Community Development
item.description: For Rivers State, Rivers State University (RSU) don make big move to improve digital capacity for the host community by officially opening the Nkpolu-Oroworukwo ICT Centre on Wednesday, May 6, 2026. I...

item.title: Kenya Power Announces Planned Power Outages in 8 Counties for Thursday, May 14
item.description: Kenya Power and Lighting Company (KPLC) has issued a notice on planned electricity interruptions affecting eight counties tomorrow, Thursday, May 14. Residents in parts of Nairobi, including South B, ...

item.title: Mayon Volcano releases pyroclastic flows causing ashfall in nearby towns
item.description: Mayon Volcano in Albay emitted lava flow collapse-fed pyroclastic density currents (PDCs) yesterday, causing ashfall in several areas including Camalig, Legazpi, and Sto. Domingo, according to the Phi...

item.title: Former Police Chief Baba Pulls Out of Yobe APC Governorship Contest
item.description: The former Inspector-General of Police, Usman Alkali Baba, don officially withdraw from the race to become the All Progressives Congress (APC) governorship candidate for Yobe State. Baba announce am f...

item.title: Fayose Pledges PDP Support for Oyebanji and Tinubu Re-election Despite Party Differences
item.description: Former Ekiti State Governor, Ayo Fayose, don talk say him still dey inside Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) but e go support Governor Biodun Oyebanji for re-election come June 20, 2026, and President Bo...

item.title: Legal Aid Initiative by Mama Samia Concludes Long-Standing Land Dispute
item.description: In Tanzania, the Mama Samia Legal Aid Campaign has successfully brought an end to a land dispute that had lasted for 12 years. The initiative, aimed at providing legal assistance to affected communiti...

item.title: Carney’s government faces challenges maintaining majority amid potential MP departures
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberal government is navigating a precarious position with a very slim majority in the House of Commons. With MPs Jonathan Wilkinson and Nathaniel Erskine-Smith expected ...

item.title: Johannesburg father mourns four-year-old twins lost in shack fire
item.description: In Langlaagte, Johannesburg, four-year-old twins Isibabalo and Isiphiwe Xakwe tragically died in a shack fire at the Zamimpilo informal settlement. Their father, Abdul Ibrahim, recalls their last word...

item.title: Innisfil team of dogs set to compete for Canada in international flyball competition
item.description: A team of dogs from Innisfil is preparing to represent Canada at the Flyball Open World Cup, a prestigious international dog sport competition. Flyball is a fast-paced relay race where teams of four d...

item.title: President Ramaphosa Announces Judicial Appointments to Bolster Court Capacity
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has announced a fresh set of judicial appointments aimed at enhancing the efficiency and capacity of South Africa’s courts. These appointments, made in line with section 174(...
